title: Decussocarpus mannii
taxonomy: Decussocarpus mannii is classified as a species of tree in the family Podocarpaceae, which is a family of coniferous plants. This family is part of the order Pinales and class Pinopsida. Decussocarpus mannii is native to New Caledonia and is a member of the genus Decussocarpus, which is a small group of conifers. The classification of Decussocarpus mannii reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of this species.

The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and act as an anti-inflammatory agent.
